comment: FR-SPD-002 speed-ramp frame: a 1x -> 4x time-remap curve (keyframes 0->0, 1/2->1/2, 1->5/2) over synthetic media whose green channel encodes the frame index at 96 fps. Sampling t=2/3 inside the 4x segment must decode source frame 112. Dropping the curve (1x) decodes frame 64 and flattening it to its constant-rate chord (5/2x) decodes frame 160 - measured failures maxDeltaE=22.2 and 30.3 against this reference. Tolerance 10 is ~2x the measured cross-machine ProRes/color-conversion noise floor (maxDeltaE 4.614 on the CI runner vs 0.000 locally) while the weakest wrong-variant signal is ~2x the tolerance; the repo's usual decode-path tolerance of 25 would swallow the dropped-curve signal, hence the tighter budget. The ramp is steepened to 1x -> 4x because a strict 1x -> 2x curve mathematically caps the constant-chord divergence below this noise-safe margin; the 1x -> 2x spec example is still exercised exactly by the FR-SPD-002 constant-speed-equivalence and piecewise unit tests and by the golden-audio time-remap-ramp fixture.
